title: leptoderris coriacea
intro: Leptoderris coriacea, also known as the red alder or coral bush, is a deciduous shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Leptoderris coriacea include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
